data.title: Two-Factor Authentication
data.description: Google enforces two-factor authentication to enhance user account security.
data.technical_spec: Supports hardware security keys and mobile authentication methods.

data.title: Data Encryption
data.description: Data at rest and in transit is encrypted to protect user privacy and integrity.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AES-256 encryption for data storage and TLS 1.3 for data transmission.

data.title: Threat Detection
data.description: Advanced threat detection systems are in place to identify and neutralize potential risks.
data.technical_spec: Employs Google Cloud's Security Command Center and machine learning algorithms.

data.title: Automated Patch Management
data.description: Patches are automatically applied to mitigate zero-day vulnerabilities promptly.
data.technical_spec: Uses continuous delivery techniques to ensure systems are up-to-date.

data.title: Firewalls
data.description: Multiple layers of firewalls safeguard Google’s infrastructure from intrusion.
data.technical_spec: Google Cloud Armor and VPC firewalls secure network perimeters.
